gap_visible.note: 7th drain-closure agreement case. Running tally: AGREE 7 (V12, V13, V18, V19, V22, V23, V26), DIVERGE 5 (V11, V14, V16, V17, V20).

endpoint_definition_disagreement.analysis: Second instance of the V18-pattern endpoint disagreement. Claude's completion is ~16s later than GPT-4o's. GPT-4o explicitly timestamps 'both suture ends appear fully cut at about 147.2s absolute' and 'first instrument becomes visible at about 8.0s.' Claude's later stop may be including final frame inspection after cutter withdrawal. RECOMMENDATION: formalize the 'last frame of cutter dividing suture' definition in the scoring prompt.

penalty_extra_disagreement.analysis: 4th rubric-scope disagreement (V20, V22, V26, V23). Recurring pattern.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: V23 is the 2nd-widest FLS disagreement in V-series (Δ18.8) after V18 (Δ17.4). Both endpoint and penalty-scope disagreements contribute. Qualitative rubric is in full agreement. If rubric-strict scoring is used (no out-of-rubric penalties, cutter-stop endpoint), GPT-4o's 457.8 is the correct value. Midpoint 448.4 is the conservative reconciled value.

systematic_findings.endpoint_pattern_recurs: V23 is the second documented case of the V18 endpoint-definition pattern. Both V18 and V23 have ΔFLS >17 driven entirely by where the scoring clock stops. This is now a repeatable systematic issue, not a one-off.
systematic_findings.drain_agreement_set: V23 is the 7th drain-closure agreement. The divergence pattern is now clearly a minority outcome (5 of 12 V-series videos). Worth retiring the 'Claude drain-gap bias' hypothesis as a general rule.
systematic_findings.phase_pattern_third_throw_as_absorber: V23 continues the pattern of GPT-4o absorbing late manipulation into third_throw (61.8s) while Claude distributes it across cut phase. Third_throw + cut: GPT-4o 67.6s vs Claude (not directly comparable without full breakdown).
